nodes.name: opsx-apply.md
nodes.summary: OpenCode command definition for opsx-apply: implements tasks from an OpenSpec change by processing the implementation workflow and executing code changes.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 112 non-empty lines defining the agent's behavior, context requirements, and execution steps for applying OpenSpec changes.

nodes.name: opsx-archive.md
nodes.summary: OpenCode command definition for opsx-archive: archives a completed OpenSpec change after implementation, finalizing and moving the change artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 109 non-empty lines defining the archival workflow for completed OpenSpec changes.

nodes.name: opsx-explore.md
nodes.summary: OpenCode command definition for opsx-explore: enter explore mode as a thinking partner for investigating problems, clarifying requirements, and brainstorming before or during a change.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 125 non-empty lines and 9 headed sections covering stance, OpenSpec awareness, context checking, and guardrails for the exploration workflow.

nodes.name: opsx-propose.md
nodes.summary: OpenCode command definition for opsx-propose: quickly propose a new OpenSpec change with all artifacts (design, specs, tasks) generated in a single step.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 80 non-empty lines defining the one-step proposal creation process for new OpenSpec changes.

nodes.name: opsx-sync.md
nodes.summary: OpenCode command definition for opsx-sync: syncs delta specs from an OpenSpec change to the main specs without archiving the change itself.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 94 non-empty lines defining the delta-to-main spec synchronization workflow.

nodes.name: speckit.analyze.md
nodes.summary: Speckit analyze command: performs non-destructive cross-artifact consistency and quality analysis across spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md after task generation.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 172 non-empty lines and 25 headed sections covering initialization, artifact loading, semantic model building, 6 detection passes (duplication, ambiguity, underspecification, constitution alignment, coverage gaps, inconsistency), severity assignment, and remediation.

nodes.name: speckit.checklist.md
nodes.summary: Speckit checklist command: generates custom checklists ('unit tests for English') based on user requirements for feature validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 287 non-empty lines and 7 headed sections including pre-execution checks, execution steps, example checklist types, anti-examples, and post-execution checks.

nodes.name: speckit.clarify.md
nodes.summary: Speckit clarify command: identifies underspecified areas in the current feature spec by asking up to 5 highly targeted clarification questions and encoding answers back into the spec.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 231 non-empty lines and 6 headed sections covering pre-execution checks, clarification outline, mandatory post-execution hooks, and completion reporting.

nodes.name: speckit.constitution.md
nodes.summary: Speckit constitution command: creates or updates the project constitution from interactive or provided principle inputs, keeping all dependent templates in sync.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 119 non-empty lines and 4 headed sections for defining and maintaining project constitutional principles.

nodes.name: speckit.implement.md
nodes.summary: Speckit implement command: executes the implementation plan by processing and executing all tasks defined in tasks.md for the current feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 173 non-empty lines and 6 headed sections covering pre-execution checks, task execution outline, mandatory post-execution hooks, and completion reporting.

nodes.name: speckit.plan.md
nodes.summary: Speckit plan command: executes the implementation planning workflow to generate design artifacts (plan.md) including phases for research, design, and contracts.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 129 non-empty lines and 10 headed sections covering two phases (Phase 0: Outline & Research, Phase 1: Design & Contracts) with post-execution hooks.

nodes.name: speckit.specify.md
nodes.summary: Speckit specify command: creates or updates the feature specification from a natural language feature description with section requirements and success criteria guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 266 non-empty lines and 10 headed sections covering specification writing guidelines, section requirements, AI generation rules, and success criteria formulation.

nodes.name: speckit.tasks.md
nodes.summary: Speckit tasks command: generates actionable, dependency-ordered tasks.md for the feature based on available design artifacts with checklist format and phase structure.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 170 non-empty lines and 10 headed sections covering task generation rules, required checklist format, task organization, and phase structure guidelines.

nodes.name: speckit.taskstoissues.md
nodes.summary: Speckit taskstoissues command: converts existing tasks into actionable, dependency-ordered GitHub issues for the feature based on available design artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown command template with 77 non-empty lines and 4 headed sections covering GitHub issue conversion with pre-execution and post-execution checks.
